title: Building a referral engine that runs without you
dek: Hoping for referrals is not a strategy. Engineering them is.
heroAlt: Building a referral engine that runs without you

blocks.html: Ask agents where business comes from and they say referrals. Ask what their referral process is and the room goes quiet. For most, referral strategy means doing decent work and hoping someone mentions it. Hope is not an engine.

An engine has parts: a moment where you earn the mention, a moment where you make asking easy, and a loop that rewards the behavior so it repeats. Remove the hoping, install the parts, and referrals shift from pleasant surprise to predictable channel.

blocks.text: Great service earns the referral. A system collects it.

items.title: The peak moment.
items.html: Move-in day, not signing day, is when goodwill spikes. A small thoughtful gesture that day is what gets described to friends verbatim.

items.title: The frictionless ask.
items.html: Thirty days in: who is the next person in your world making a move? I will take care of them the way I took care of you. Specific, warm, effortless to answer.

items.title: The closed loop.
items.html: When a referral lands, report back and say thank you like you mean it. People repeat what gets acknowledged.

blocks.html: Design your move-in touch this week. It does not need to be expensive. It needs to be specific enough that the client tells someone about it. That retelling is the engine turning over.
Course 5 maps the entire client journey, with the referral machinery built into every stage instead of bolted on at the end.

blocks.lead: Course 5 includes the complete referral framework.
